note: Hand-curated seed signals for v0.1.0 cold start. Sourced from the Subvurs Quantum Curator corpus (`/Users/mvm/Desktop/quantum-curator/data/curator.db` as of 2026-05-25) plus the original three anchor papers. Each entry is classified by evidence_class per CLAUDE.md §3: `hardware` = peer-reviewed result or unambiguous hardware demonstration; `theory` = arXiv preprint without hardware execution; `roadmap` = vendor blog or press release describing intent / planned milestones. Roadmap entries will be capped by RoadmapWeightCapGate when it lands in v0.2; until then their normalized_value still contributes but confidence is set low. Normalized values follow the axis-1 anchor map in `docs/SIGNAL_CATALOG.md` (distance ≤ 3 → 0.00; distance 4-5 → 0.35; distance 6-11 → 0.50; distance 12-17 → 0.60; distance > 17 → 0.70; 4-9 logical qubits → 0.55-0.65; ≥ 10 logical qubits → 0.70-0.85).

signals.title: Google: surface code distance-7 logical qubit below threshold
signals.summary: Google Quantum AI demonstrated a distance-7 surface code logical qubit operating below the threshold for fault-tolerance, with logical error rate decreasing as distance increases.

signals.title: IBM: surface code distance-5 logical qubit on Heron
signals.summary: IBM demonstrated a distance-5 surface code logical qubit on a Heron-family processor.

signals.title: Quantinuum: 12 logical qubits with bb-style qLDPC code
signals.summary: Quantinuum H2 demonstrated multiple logical qubits encoded with a qLDPC (bivariate-bicycle-style) code, with measured logical error rates competitive with surface code at lower physical-qubit overhead.

signals.title: Microsoft + Atom Computing: 24 entangled logical qubits on neutral atoms
signals.summary: Microsoft and Atom Computing announced creation and entanglement of 24 logical qubits from neutral-atom physical qubits. Vendor-announced; awaiting independent peer-reviewed confirmation.

signals.title: Microsoft Majorana 1 topological qubit processor announcement
signals.summary: Microsoft announced Majorana 1, described as a quantum processor unit powered by a Topological Core intended to scale to a million qubits on a single chip. Topological qubit claims have been contested by parts of the community; this entry is included as a roadmap signal at low confidence.

signals.title: Infleqtion: 12 logical qubits on Sqale neutral-atom processor for biomarker discovery
signals.summary: Infleqtion reported execution of biomarker discovery algorithms using 12 logical qubits on its Sqale neutral-atom quantum computer, in a Wellcome-Leap-funded Q4Bio project with UChicago and MIT collaborators. Hardware demonstration; full peer-reviewed write-up pending.

signals.title: Pasqal: error-detected logical qubits outperform physical on differential-equation solver
signals.summary: Pasqal published application-level hardware research (with Université Paris-Saclay and Institut d'Optique) showing error-detected logical qubits outperforming their physical counterparts on a quantum-kernel differential-equation solver running on the neutral-atom platform.

signals.title: Innsbruck + Aachen: fault-tolerant Grover on 3 logical qubits without mid-circuit measurements
signals.summary: Researchers in Innsbruck and Aachen experimentally demonstrated on a trapped-ion quantum processor that a universal fault-tolerant quantum algorithm — Grover's search on three logical qubits — can be executed without mid-circuit measurements, removing one of the largest practical hurdles in quantum error correction on encoded qubits.

signals.title: AI-based pre-decoders for surface codes
signals.summary: Scalable AI-based pre-decoder for surface codes that performs local, parallel error correction with low decoding runtimes, removing the majority of physical errors before passing residual syndromes to a downstream decoder. Algorithm and simulation results only; no hardware execution.

signals.title: SAT-based EDA kernel for surface-code logical operations
signals.summary: Design-automation framework for fault-tolerant quantum computers based on surface codes and lattice surgery, identifying logical operations with low space-time cost and verifying their fault tolerance. Compilation theory, no hardware demonstration.

signals.title: Optimizing logical mappings for the Gross qLDPC code
signals.summary: Compilation study for the Gross code, a quantum low-density parity-check code with low spatial overhead. Targets execution with low total program error rate for fault-tolerant applications. Theory only.

signals.title: Partially fault-tolerant quantum computation for megaquop applications
signals.summary: Quantum resource estimation for the space-time-efficient analog rotation (STAR) architecture targeting megaquop-scale (millions of logical operations) circuits. Establishes strengths and limitations of the partially-fault-tolerant approach. Resource-estimate theory only.

signals.title: Adaptive loss-tolerant syndrome measurements for FTEC under qubit loss
signals.summary: Loss-tolerant syndrome-measurement scheme for fault-tolerant error correction under qubit loss, with optimized syndrome measurement sequences. Targets neutral-atom architectures where qubit loss dominates over Pauli errors. Theory.

signals.title: Low-overhead fault-tolerant quantum computation by gauging logical operators
signals.summary: University of Sydney work (Dr. Dominic Williamson and collaborators) on a new approach to quantum error correction that could significantly reduce the number of physical qubits required to build large-scale fault-tolerant quantum computers, via gauging of logical operators. Theory; press-coverage entry, awaiting independent replication.

signals.title: Optimizing parallel execution of commuting Pauli product rotations under FTQC
signals.summary: Compiler heuristics for fault-tolerant quantum computation that reduce hardware-limited circuit depth by reshuffling cliques of commuting Pauli product rotations under per-qubit access-port limits (e.g. two X and two Z edges on the surface code).

signals.title: IonQ: published blueprint targeting 10,000+ physical qubits and scalable error-corrected systems
signals.summary: IonQ published a full-stack technical report describing a path to 10,000+ qubits and scalable, error-corrected quantum systems. Vendor roadmap; no hardware result attached, no peer review.

signals.title: IQM + Zurich Instruments + NVIDIA NVQLink: real-time QEC demonstrator project
signals.summary: Joint project announcement by IQM Quantum Computers, Zurich Instruments, and NVIDIA to build a real-time quantum error correction demonstrator on the NVQLink platform, targeting enterprise / datacenter deployment. No experimental results yet; project-launch announcement.
